key_insight: In neutron-irradiated tungsten, strain rate and indentation depth affect plasticity through different mechanisms: strain rate sets thermally activated flow stress, while depth controls plastic-zone size, pile-up, and GND buildup.
engineering_relevance: The calibrated strain-gradient crystal plasticity model can predict irradiation-hardened tungsten response across strain rates and loading scales, supporting constitutive design for transient and extreme environments.
method: Spherical nanoindentation on unirradiated and neutron-irradiated single-crystal tungsten across 3.2e-5 to 3.2e-3 s^-1
AFM and HR-EBSD measurements of pile-up, residual strain, and GND distributions
Strain-gradient CPFE model with thermally activated slip, GND hardening, irradiation obstacle hardening, and strain-dependent softening
evidence_signal: A single calibrated model condition was validated across all remaining strain rates without re-tuning and then used to independently vary strain rate and indentation depth in simulation.
watch_item: Transferability is demonstrated for the studied tungsten conditions, but the model still depends on calibration against one experimental state and may need retuning for different irradiation states or temperatures.
paper_title: Decoupling Strain-Rate Sensitivity and Deformation Length Scale Effects in Neutron-Irradiated Tungsten: A Coupled Nano-Indentation, HR-EBSD and Crystal Plasticity Study
